# Requirement: FR-010

- Title: Deliver responsive navigation menu for mobile devices
- Status: Active

## Requirement Statement

The system shall provide a mobile-friendly navigation experience where the top-level menu adapts gracefully to small screen sizes, ensuring all navigation items remain accessible and visually appealing without horizontal overflow or wrapping.

## Rationale

On smartphone screens, the current navigation menu items do not fit in a single line, causing visual clutter and poor user experience. A responsive design ensures the site remains professional and usable across all device sizes, improving accessibility and user satisfaction.

## Acceptance Conditions

- On screens smaller than 768px (tablet breakpoint), the navigation transforms into a mobile-friendly layout.
- A hamburger menu (☰) or similar collapsible trigger is visible on mobile, replacing the horizontal menu bar.
- Tapping the hamburger menu reveals all navigation items in a vertical overlay or dropdown.
- The mobile menu includes the language switcher if present.
- Navigation items remain clickable and properly spaced in the mobile layout.
- The mobile menu can be dismissed by tapping outside, using a close button, or navigating to a page.
- Desktop navigation (screens ≥768px) remains unchanged and functional.
- No horizontal scrolling is required to access navigation on any screen size.

## Success Metrics

- Navigation items fit within the viewport width on screens down to 320px.
- Mobile users can access all site sections without frustration.
- Build and responsive behavior remain consistent across browsers.
- Accessibility standards (WCAG) are maintained for mobile interactions.

## Non-Goals

- Redesigning the desktop navigation layout.
- Adding advanced mobile features like swipe gestures.
- Changing the site's responsive breakpoints beyond standard mobile/tablet/desktop.

## Constraints

- Preserve the existing Astro component structure and Tailwind CSS styling approach.
- Maintain accessibility features (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation) in the mobile implementation.
- Ensure the mobile menu does not interfere with page content or cause layout shifts.
- Keep the implementation lightweight and performant.
